text: chapter number four. And through the summer here, we have spent Sunday evenings, not last Sunday evening, but several Sunday evenings. We have been studying through a series that I've entitled Ask the Pastor. We gave forms and allowed you to submit questions. Originally, we were planning to go through the summer with the number of questions submitted. We'll go through the fall as well. And it'll be a Sunday night series here for a number of months. And so we're looking forward to it again this evening to getting back to the series. The question that was asked this evening that I'll try to answer is where did Cain get his wife? And Genesis chapter number four is where we'll go this evening and begin in that text of scripture in order to answer the question. And perhaps you've been asked this question nearly a hundred years ago now. There was a famous trial down in Tennessee, the Scopes Trial. Many of you might be familiar with that. And you've read about it. I'm sure that brother Michael has made some study perhaps of the Scopes Trial. And you see some things there. And it's kind of interesting. There was an entire line of questioning with regard to this very question that we're dealing with this evening on where Cain got his wife. Someone said this, that she is the most talked about woman in history, though we do not know her name. And because of so many folks that have asked the question, have sought really to undermine, as I would say it, many that are typically asking this question are really simply undermining the authority and accuracy of scripture. And really that's the attack of the enemy, right? God has given us his word. God wants us to know his word. God wants us to believe his word and trust his word. But we know that the enemy is constantly trying to attack the word of God, undermine the authority and the accuracy of the word of God. And if he can cause there to be a measure of doubt in someone's mind and their heart with regard to the accuracy of this book, he gains really an advantage. And we must not allow him to gain an advantage. We know that God's word is true. Every word of God is pure. We know that it's perfect. It's been given by inspiration. It's been divinely preserved. And God has given us everything that we need right here in the Bible. We believe not only the inspiration and the preservation of God's word, but I believe, and it's so important, and maybe something that we don't speak about as often enough, but that is the sufficiency of scripture. God's word is sufficient. God has given us everything that we need to know. He's given to us in his word, and we're thankful for that. Modern day skeptics raise this question, and I believe that we ought to be prepared to give an answer. Notice, if you will, in Genesis 4, and this is where they run to, verse 16 and 17, of course, after the situation with Cain and Abel is recorded in the beginning of Genesis 4, and the punishment, if you will, that was given out to Cain. He, in fact, says that his punishment is greater than he can bear. But if you notice in verse number 16, it says that Cain went out from the presence of the Lord, and what a sad statement that is. Not really the scope of our study this evening, but there's no record whatsoever of Cain ever returning to the presence of God. And it says that he went out from the presence of the Lord and dwelt in the land of Nod on the east of Eden. The name of the city where he goes, the land of Nod, literally means a land of wandering or exile. And then the Bible says this in verse number 17, and Cain knew his wife, and she conceived and bare Enoch, and he built a city and called the name of the city after the name of his son, Enoch. Now, as we begin to think about the attack of scripture, when these skeptics would ask the question and try to undermine, again, the authority and the accuracy of the word of God, I really believe that the approach that they're taking, and if you study what they say and the entirety of what they're dealing with, what they're trying to cause you to believe is that there must have been other human beings created other than Adam and Eve. It didn't really start with just Adam and Eve. And so there's other colonies of people that are on the earth. And this really becomes a major problem when you accept something like that because it has a direct correspondence to the gospel, and we'll deal with that at the end of the message this evening, and do trust it to be a blessing to you. But the reality is that they would have us to believe that there's no possible way that all of it just started with two people, Adam and Eve. And this is a really sad situation. In fact, I was in the presence of some people that I've known for years, and no one from our church, but I was in the presence, sitting around having conversation. And some folks that were at that table were folks that grew up in church. They'd been in church their whole lives. A couple of the folks around the table had graduated from Bible college, and one of them piped up and they made this statement, and actually the individual that made the statement was a graduate of Bible college. And here's what they said. The question that we must really answer is how many Adams were there? And really what was happening in that individual's life is they were just buying into the false teaching, the undermining of the authority of Scripture, and believing that, well, yeah, I mean, Cain had to get away from somewhere, and so, yeah, I guess it kind of makes sense that there were other groups of people. You know, just go back to the Bible and read what God's word says, and trust what God's word says, and God will help us to have the discernment that we need. And so as we think about the question that is raised, and they begin to approach it with regard to Cain's wife, again, I believe that it's dangerous because of the impact on the gospel, the direct correlation throughout the word of God with regard to the fall of man, the sin nature of man, it all going back to Adam, and of course Eve being in transgression. You look at the New Testament and the references to Adam and Eve. And so tonight, I hope that through answering the question, very simply, we can answer the question, where did Cain get his wife? But I hope this evening that I could prove to you some additional things with regard to really helping us to be able to have ourselves settled and grounded in the faith. And really, that's the objective. We understand the great commission that's been given to us as a local church, as New Testament believers. We've been given the commission to go out and preach the gospel to every creature, baptizing them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost, and then teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you. And that process of discipleship is so imperative that the people of God would be settled and grounded in the faith, that we would not have some skeptic, some individual that's just seeking to undermine the authority and the accuracy of scripture that would come to us and say, oh yeah, well, tell me about where Cain got his wife. And then all of a sudden, everything that we've believed just kind of falls apart because we're not settled, we're not grounded. We do not have the ability to give the answer of the hope that lieth within us. And our goal, our objective, is not only to evangelize the world, but to edify the believer. That word edify was a word that was used very commonly with regard to building and construction. It means to build up and that we would be settled and grounded in the faith. And so a few statements this evening as we go through the message. Number one, I would say this, the scriptures clearly teach us that God created Adam and Eve to start the human race. There's no other areas of scripture where there would be any indication, not even the slightest indication of any other human beings being created. In fact, the scriptures make it abundantly clear that it was only Adam and Eve. Look in Genesis one in verse number 26 through 28 and verses that we're familiar with, but notice what the Bible says. And God said in verse 26, let us make man in our image after our likeness and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the fowl of the air and over the cattle and over all the earth and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth, verse 27. So God created man in his own image and the image of God created he him, male and female created he them and God blessed them and God said unto them, be fruitful and multiply and replenish the earth and subdue it and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the fowl of the air and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth. And we see the record on day six where God created mankind. Of course, God gives us further insight in chapter number two with regard to some details and we'll look at that here in just a moment, but I do wanna make you aware of, and you already are, but just to be mindful this evening, that in verse 28, God said to them, be fruitful and multiply. This was a command that was given to Adam and Eve. We also find it's a command that's given to Noah, his wife, his sons, and their wives after the flood and I believe that they did fulfill and God blessed. See, God is the giver of life and God allowed them to multiply, to be able to fulfill the command that he had given. Of course, the ability to have perhaps multiple children at one time with regard to twins or triplets and so on and so forth and God was blessing as he had given. in them this command. But I want you to notice in chapter number two, and I'm gonna read a number of verses in chapter number two, and I want you to notice as we read these verses of scripture how that God always speaks in the singular with regard to creation of man. Notice in verse number five, in every plant of the field before it was in the earth, in every herb of the field before it grew, for the Lord God had not caused its rain upon the earth, and there was not a man to till the ground. It's pretty straightforward, is it not? God's not saying there wasn't a man here. There's not a man. So it's not that there were other people that were created somewhere else that Cain is able to travel to Nod and find a group of people there, no. The Bible says that there was not a man to till the ground. Notice in verse number eight, it says in the Lord God planted a garden eastward in Eden, and there he put the man whom he had formed. It's Adam, singular, the man that he had formed. You see in verse number 15, the Bible says in the Lord God took the man and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it, and the Lord God commanded the man, saying, of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat, but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it, for in the day that thou eatest thereof, thou shalt surely die. In verse number 18, it says the Lord God said it is not good that the man should be alone. So what's God gonna do? I will make him and help me for him. Amen, verse 19, and out of the ground, the Lord God formed every beast of the field and every fowl of the air and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them and what sort of Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof, and Adam gave names to all cattle, to all, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field, but for Adam there was not found in help meat for him. If God had created other peoples in other places of the earth, he could have just brought somebody to Adam, but that's not how God did it. God formed Adam, the man, and then, of course, he created Eve. It says in verse 21, and the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and he slept, and he took one of his ribs and closed up the flesh instead thereof, and the rib which the Lord God had taken from man made he a woman and brought her unto the man. And Adam said, this is now bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh. She shall be called woman because she was taken out of man. As we read these verses of scripture, we see that it's very clear that God uses consistently the singular in describing the man and the woman, and it gives us great, solid evidence with regard to the truth that God created Adam and Eve to start the human race. Jesus is going to make a reference here to the beginning of creation, and it's important that we see how Jesus is making this reference and dealing with the answer to those that were coming to him. So then they are no more twain but one flesh, what therefore God hath joined together. Let not man put asunder. And so God is going all the way back to the beginning of time, to creation, and of course, in that text of Scripture in Genesis chapter two, we're gonna be in Acts 17 here in just a moment, but in Genesis chapter number two, after Adam says, this is now bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh, God says this, therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother and shall cleave unto his wife, and they shall be one flesh. And you see that the Lord Jesus Christ is referencing back to creation, to the beginning, and to the male and female, to the truth of creation with Adam and Eve. In Acts chapter number 17, the Bible speaks to us about the truth of there being one human race. And we're thankful that God's word is clear with regard to the teaching. I've never been asked those questions, but this question about Cain comes up, and I think it comes up for this reason, and look again at Genesis chapter four in verse number 17. I want you to understand that the Bible does not tell us, again, let the Bible speak for itself, and don't read into what it says. It does not say that Cain found a wife, married a wife, and then, no, it says that Cain knew his wife, and she conceived, and bare a son, his name being Enoch. We simply accept it as being what it is, truth, and it being God giving us a record. Now, notice that when we answer the question, it must be that Cain married his sister. Today, and obviously as you look at even the laws that God gave in Leviticus 18 through 20, there would be concern with regard to biological deformities. And so when God created Adam and Eve, there was no problem for their children to marry their siblings and to have marriages and to reproduce and so on and so forth. We often would say, and we understand, and as these recipients would have known the Old Testament, and they'd be understanding of what Paul is saying, that that one man is Adam. Verse 13, For until the law, sin was in the world, but sin is not imputed when there is no law. Nevertheless, death reigned from Adam to Moses, even over them that had not sinned after the similitude of Adam's transgression, who is the figure of him that was to come. But not as the offense, so also is the free gift. For if through the offense of one many be dead, who is the one? It's Adam. So if through the offense of one many be dead, much more the grace of God and the gift by grace, which is by one man, Jesus Christ, hath abounded unto many. And not as it was by one that sinned, so is the gift. For the judgment was by one to condemnation, but the free gift is of many offenses unto justification. For if by one man's offense death reigned by one, much more they which receive abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousness shall reign in life by one, Jesus Christ. Therefore, as by the offense of one judgment came upon all men to condemnation, even so by the righteousness of one the free gift came upon all men unto justification of life. For as by one man's disobedience many were made sinners, so by the obedience of one shall many be made righteous. Moreover, the law entered that the offense might abound, but where sin abounded, grace did much more abound, that as sin hath reigned unto death, even so might grace reign through righteousness unto eternal life by Jesus Christ our Lord." If we accept the false teaching that these would seek to lead us to believe when they ask the question, where did Cain get his wife, and they want to lead down this other path of other peoples being created, it has a direct impact on the gospel, direct. And that's why it's so critical. Notice in 1 Corinthians 15. Notice what is said here, and we know that this chapter, 1 Corinthians 15, is the chapter that we run to with regard to the resurrection. It's the great resurrection chapter. And I want you to see what the Bible says. Again, the authority that God gives us with regard to the first man, and strictly speaking of God creating Adam and Eve and telling them, be fruitful and multiply, and then of course the truth of the gospel. It says in verse number 45, and so it is written, the first man Adam was made a living soul, the last Adam was made a quickening spirit. Who's the last Adam? That's Jesus Christ. It says this in verse number 46, howbeit that was not first which is spiritual, but that which is natural, and afterward that which is spiritual. For the first man is of the earth, earthy. The second man is the Lord from heaven. As is the earthy, such are they also that are earthy. And as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly. And as we have borne the image of the earthy, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly. Now this I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, neither doth corruption inherit incorruption. And then we rejoice in the truth of the resurrection, that Christ is risen, He is the firstfruits, Paul has established that back in the middle section of 1 Corinthians 15. And then notice how it applies to us. He says in verse 51, Behold, I show you a mystery. We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed in a moment. And the twinkling of an eye at the last trump, for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed. For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality. Well, where did the corruption and the mortality come from? The first Adam. Where does the incorruption and the immortality come from? The last Adam, Christ. It's the truth of the Gospel. And so this evening, what some might look at is, what's the big deal? So what if there were... No, no, no, it's a big deal. It is absolutely a big deal because of the impact that it would have on the Gospel. And we must understand, what is the goal of Satan, ultimately? To keep men blinded. Right? That's the ultimate goal of Satan. He will use any means, right? He will use any means to keep men blinded. And what a tragedy for those that have been given... that have been born again, that have been given the truth of the Word of God, to allow some skeptics to come in and to say, well, this isn't really an important issue. This doesn't really matter. We can just be more open-minded and we can be more inclusive and so on and so forth. And you become so inclusive that by the end, you don't stand for anything. And that's really the end goal. Listen, biblical Christianity is not pragmatic. It's not, you know, the ends justifies the means. Biblical Christianity is, what does God say? And that settles it. And so this evening, Cain had to have married his sister. If you believe Nice, I wouldn't argue with you. I believe he married his sister. I have no problem with it whatsoever. Perfectly fine. There was not another group of people over in the land of Nod that God had created that are separate, that that's where he goes and finds a wife and so on and so forth. No, God created Adam and Eve. Eve is the mother of all living. All living. Bible college professor said this very deep. All means all. And that's all that all means. And so when God says all, he means all. He didn't mean some. He didn't mean most. He didn't mean many. He meant all. And so this evening, we answer the question, I trust there'll be a blessing to you that helps us to be able to be settled and grounded. If you've not been confronted with the question, you may soon be. I've had the question asked of me many times over the years, and it's been the greatest, I guess, impact of just really causing me to be sad is when it's asked of me from someone that has been taught and given the truth of the word of God and has bought into the false teaching of why does it even matter? It matters because God tells us and because of the impact on the gospel. Let's pray. Father, we love you. We thank you for loving us. We thank you for the word of God, the truth contained therein. And Lord, I pray that as we have taught through these messages here on Sunday evenings of Ask the Pastor, I understand it's been more teachy than preachy. And Lord, I pray that you'd help us to just have our faith increased, to be settled and grounded, to be strengthened, to no longer be in a place where we'd be tossed to and fro with any wind of doctrine, that we would understand the ultimate goal of false teachers and the desire of Satan to blind the minds of those that have not believed, knowing that he'll use any means possible. Lord, I pray that this evening that you'd help us to be grounded, settled, help us to be encouraged as we've studied your word. We thank you for giving us your word, and we just pray that you'd bless the remainder of our evening. We'll thank you for it. In Christ's name we pray. Would you stand with me? Heads are bowed, eyes are closed. Give an opportunity for a invitation this evening. Maybe just wanna come and thank God for his word. And just so thankful that God's given us the Bible, and that the Bible has the answers, and maybe God spoke to your heart through some other way throughout the day. The altar is open as Kimberly plays. Thank you.

20, and of course in Genesis chapter number 12, it's when they go to Egypt, and

of verse number three, but God. God intervened, God protected, and God
appeared to Abimelech by night, and he says at the end of verse number
three, for she is a man's wife, and told Abimelech that he was but a dead man
because of what had taken place. Abimelech believed God. Abimelech

people play on, these skeptics that come to us today and say, so where did
